explicit_text: I worked for the Atlantic Coast Line Railroad for many years, starting on the section gang and later as a watchman at a busy crossing. My duties included stopping traffic when trains passed, and I took pride in keeping the crossing safe with no accidents since I took over. I remember working on the extra gang cutting rails and cooking for workers, and now I live in a section house along the main line near Lake Wire. The railroad has been central to my life and livelihood.

explicit_text: I was born in Jefferson County, Florida, on December 5th, 1866, and my parents were farmers there. I ran away from home as a boy because of my stepmother’s treatment but later returned as a man. I married my first wife, Rosetta Turner, in Monticello, Jefferson County, in 1901, and I also worked on farms there with my father. Jefferson County is where my roots and early life are deeply planted.
